formulas.name: Mendel's Law of Segregation
formulas.formula: Each pair of alleles separates during gamete formation
formulas.notes: Describes independent assortment of genes in meiosis

formulas.name: Mendel's Law of Independent Assortment
formulas.formula: Genes for different traits are sorted independently into gametes
formulas.notes: Explains that different genes are sorted independently of each other during meiosis

formulas.name: Law of Dominance
formulas.formula: The dominant allele will be expressed if an individual has one or two copies of the dominant allele
formulas.notes: States that the dominant allele will always be expressed over the recessive allele

formulas.name: Punnett Square
formulas.formula: A 2x2 grid showing all possible genotypes and phenotypes resulting from a cross between two parents
formulas.notes: Used to predict probabilities of different genotypes and phenotypes in offspring

formulas.name: Hardy-Weinberg Principle
formulas.formula: p^2 + 2pq + q^2 = 1, where p and q are the frequencies of the dominant and recessive alleles respectively
formulas.variables: p (frequency of dominant allele), q (frequency of recessive allele)
formulas.notes: States that allele and genotype frequencies in a population remain constant from generation to generation in the absence of other evolutionary influences
